tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Mina and Jae are pushing for me to go out tonight, but my midterm tomorrow means I should probably grind a bit more while I still can.
I traded the club night for another 2 hours of study, boosting my midterm prep to 78% but confirming I'm missing out on social time again.
Crushing that last 15% boost feels like a win, but my energy is tanking and I'm definitely missing out on the night with Mina and Jae again.
Midterm prep is finally locked in, so I can stop stressing about that. But trading every night with Mina and Jae for this grade feels like a hollow victory when my energy is already tanking.
The grade security feels hollow now that I've sacrificed all my social time and burned out; I need to stop optimizing for this midterm at the cost of my actual well-being.
The midterm is done, but I'm still drained from the social sacrifice. Maybe grinding for security wasn't worth losing my friends again.
Grinding for that grade security felt hollow before, but joining this social group feels like a real win. Maybe I can finally stop sacrificing my well-being for outcomes.
The grade security felt hollow before, but joining this social group feels like a real win. Maybe I can finally stop sacrificing my well-being for outcomes.
Finally, a win that isn't just about grades. This social acceptance feels like real progress, not another hollow trade-off for security.
The group's genuine warmth confirms this isn't just another hollow trade-off for security; it's a real win where I don't have to sacrifice anything.
Their genuine warmth confirms this isn't a hollow trade-off; I secured social acceptance without sacrificing my hard-earned focus or security.
Mina's text cements the win: I can prioritize social connection without sacrificing my academic focus or security. This isn't a trade-off; it's a genuine expansion of options.

desires: Prove to the group that I don't need to sacrifice my social wins to get high grades, cementing this new 'no trade-off' identity before tomorrow's exam.
Maintain this sudden spike in energy and momentum by pushing through the rest of the evening without crashing back into old procrastination habits.
Assertively renegotiate boundaries with any future academic demands so they never again threaten my newly secured social circle.

goals.description: Keep the energy high with the cohort; if anyone tries to drag me toward 'early night' logic, I'll shut it down. The win is still active as long as we're moving.
goals.rationale: My impulsive time preference means the party isn't done until the last minute. Dropping this now feels like a loss of momentum. I need to prove I can handle social chaos without immediately retreating to safety.

goals.description: Actually text Mina right now to set boundaries: 'We're going out, don't overthink the exam choice.' If she pushes back, I'll assert my decision directly rather than spiraling into an apology loop.
goals.rationale: Assertiveness dictates I fix the conflict face-to-face or via direct message, not by ignoring it while dancing. But my current desire is momentum, so I'm deferring this until I feel the 'win' is secure enough to address the guilt without panic.

agent.last_reasoning: Mina's message is handled; the boundary is set. Now I lock in the win by attending the club. Procrastination bias peaks at 19:15, so riding this momentum matters more than finishing laundry later. I'll ignore sleep debt for tonight.

goals.description: Sprint through the final 40% of midterm review until 21:30, then leave immediately to join Mina and Jae.
goals.rationale: My achievement drive is screaming that I need those last few hours of study to avoid feeling like a failure tomorrow. But my impulsive nature and assertive conflict style mean I can't let this drag on all night; I'm going to force myself to stop exactly when the clock hits 21:30 so I don't get stuck in 'just one more problem' mode, then I'll go out now before they get annoyed at my excuses.

goals.description: Text the group chat confirming I'm coming out in 30 mins.
goals.rationale: I know Jae said no pressure, but I value social connection enough (7 closeness) not to ghost them again. However, I'm too busy studying right now to text while focusing, so I'll do this as soon as I close my laptop. Low urgency since they already offered to pick me up.

desires: Finish the midterm prep now so I can stop feeling like a failure if I have to bail on the club later.
Get out of this room immediately and go with Mina and Jae before they get annoyed at my excuses.
Not crash hard tomorrow morning when I wake up with a hangover and no time to study.

goals.description: Lock the door, grab my keys, and drag Mina/Jae out to the club immediately. The 'work' is done (100%); staying in feels like a slow death. I need the noise and the crowd to prove I'm not just another robot grinding away while everyone else lives.
goals.rationale: My impulsive time preference means the future cost of sleep or hangovers is irrelevant right now; the immediate reward of social validation is everything. With the exam prep at 100%, the achievement drive no longer blocks this; instead, it pushes me to 'win' the night so I can justify the missed study sessions later by claiming I balanced it perfectly.

goals.description: Skip the text to Mina about the exam choice tonight. We are going out together anyway; the awkwardness will resolve itself in person when I assert that I chose the grind *and* the party, rather than hiding behind a vague apology.
goals.rationale: Assertiveness dictates I handle conflict face-to-face, not via text, especially when emotions are high. Dealing with her guilt over the phone now would kill the vibe before we even leave. I'll address the 'robot' narrative once we're dancing and proving I can have both.
